Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First I need to understand the normative process from the description The process always starts with Receive order After that Check inventory must occur before any decisions about payment or fulfillment So the first two activities should always be Receive order followed by Check inventory

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Now there are two main scenarios 1 If inventory is sufficient The sequence should be Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Package items Ship order Close order 2 If inventory is insufficient The sequence should be Receive order Check inventory Cancel order Close order

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Key constraints Authorize payment should only occur after Check inventory and before packaging Package items should only occur after authorization and before shipping Ship order should only occur after packaging and never after a cancellation Close order should be the final activity in all scenarios

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1 Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Package items Ship order Close order This matches the expected flow for sufficient inventory Receive Check Authorize Package Ship Close So no violation here

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2 Receive order Check inventory Package items Authorize payment Ship order Close order Here after Check inventory we have Package items before Authorize payment According to the constraints Authorize payment should occur before Package items because payment authorization must be done before packaging So this is a violation Specifically Package items occurs before Authorize payment which violates the rule that packaging should only occur after authorization

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 3 Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Cancel order Ship order Close order In this case after Check inventory we have Authorize payment and then Cancel order But according to the process if inventory is insufficient we should cancel immediately after checking inventory without authorizing payment Here payment is authorized before cancellation Also after Cancel order we have Ship order which should not happen because shipping should never occur after cancellation Moreover the sequence should be Cancel order Close order directly but here we have Ship order before Close order which is incorrect So multiple violations authorizing payment before cancellation and shipping after cancellation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 4 Receive order Check inventory Cancel order Close order This matches the expected flow for insufficient inventory Receive Check Cancel Close So no violation here

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 5 Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Ship order Close order Here after Authorize payment we have Ship order but there is no Package items activity According to the constraints Ship order should only occur after Package items So this is a violation because shipping happens without packaging Also the sequence should include packaging between authorization and shipping

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Now to summarize Case 1 No violation Case 2 Violation Package items before Authorize payment Case 3 Violations Authorize payment before Cancel order and Ship order after Cancel order Case 4 No violation Case 5 Violation Ship order without Package items
